rationale: The two papers are tightly aligned on haptics-to-ownership mechanisms in marketing: the citing paper’s core chain is vicarious touch  body ownership/psychological ownership  valuation, while the cited paper’s stated contribution is compatible haptic cues increasing outcomes via perceived ownership transfer. Given this direct construct/mechanism overlap (plus related boundary logic around graspability/diagnostic touch), this is plausibly more than an incidental mention and merits full relation judgment.
